page: Microsoft Azure D2as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dav6 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz with dedicated CPU allocation. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or a GPU, but it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile. The D2as_v6 exhibits strong single-core performance, placing in the top 25% for both stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core tests. However, multi-core performance is limited, with stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the bottom 25%. Memory performance is also a constraint; while large block write bandwidth is average, read-only bandwidth and overall PassMark memory scores are weak. Database operations and medium-model LLM text generation perform poorly, landing in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D2as_v6 offers a low-cost entry point for workloads that require dedicated CPU resources but do not demand high multi-threaded throughput or local storage. It is best suited for light web serving, single-threaded background tasks, software development testing, and basic application hosting. It is not recommended for database-heavy applications or intensive multi-threaded processing due to its performance bottlenecks in those areas.
description: Microsoft Azure D2as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dav6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 x86_64 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs (1 physical core, 2 threads) and 8.0 GB of RAM, with no local storage or GPU. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, ranking in the top 25% for single-core CPU tests. However, multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations are weak, with database operations falling into the bottom 10%. This resource profile makes the D2as_v6 a cost-effective option for light, single-threaded workloads, such as basic web serving, development environments, and simple application hosting, while remaining unsuitable for data-intensive or highly parallelized tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2as_v6 is a general-purpose VM featuring an AMD EPYC 9V74 CPU with 2 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM. It offers strong single-core performance but limited multi-core and database capabilities.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2as_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, optimized for light single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU instance featuring strong single-core AMD EPYC performance for light, cost-conscious general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB RAM
Strong single-core CPU performance in the top 25% of benchmarks
No local storage included, but supports Premium Storage
Weak multi-core and database performance limits heavy parallel workloads
Appropriate for light web serving, development, and single-threaded tasks
